questions.q: In which Indian state is the Great Stupa at Sanchi located?
questions.explanation: Sanchi is a town in the Raisen district of Madhya Pradesh in central India.

questions.q: The Great Stupa at Sanchi is associated with which religion?
questions.explanation: Sanchi is one of the oldest and most important Buddhist monuments in India.

questions.q: Which Mauryan emperor is credited with originally commissioning the Great Stupa at Sanchi?
questions.explanation: Emperor Ashoka commissioned the original Great Stupa at Sanchi in the 3rd century BCE.

questions.q: What is the basic shape of the main dome of the Great Stupa at Sanchi?
questions.explanation: The Great Stupa has a characteristic hemispherical (dome-shaped) form built over a relic chamber.

questions.q: What are the ornately carved gateways of the Sanchi Stupa called?
questions.explanation: The four elaborately carved gateways at Sanchi are known as toranas.

questions.q: How many ornamental gateways (toranas) surround the Great Stupa at Sanchi?
questions.explanation: Four toranas, one facing each of the cardinal directions, surround the Great Stupa.

questions.q: In roughly which century BCE was the original Sanchi Stupa first built?
questions.explanation: Ashoka commissioned the original stupa in the 3rd century BCE.

questions.q: What was the primary purpose of a stupa like the one at Sanchi?
options: A reliquary mound enshrining sacred relics
questions.explanation: A stupa is a dome-shaped reliquary monument built to enshrine sacred relics and serve as a focus of worship.

questions.q: The Great Stupa at Sanchi is also commonly referred to by what name?
questions.explanation: The main monument is officially called Stupa No. 1, popularly known as the Great Stupa.

questions.q: What is the international recognition given to the Buddhist monuments at Sanchi?
options: New Seven Wonders of the World
questions.explanation: The Buddhist Monuments at Sanchi were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1989.

questions.q: The railing (vedika) and the raised walkway around the Sanchi Stupa allow devotees to perform which ritual?
questions.explanation: The enclosing railing defines a path for pradakshina, the clockwise circumambulation of the stupa.

questions.q: Under which later dynasty were the four elaborately carved gateways (toranas) of the Great Stupa added?
questions.explanation: The richly carved toranas were added in the 1st century BCE, generally credited to the Satavahana period.

questions.q: During which empire was the original brick stupa at Sanchi later enlarged and encased in stone?
questions.explanation: In the 2nd century BCE under the Shungas the original Mauryan brick stupa was enlarged and faced with stone.

questions.q: What is the umbrella-like structure crowning the top of the Sanchi Stupa called?
questions.explanation: The top features a harmika (square railing) supporting a chhatra, a tiered stone umbrella symbolizing honor.

questions.q: In the narrative carvings on the Sanchi toranas, how is the Buddha typically represented?
options: Through symbols rather than a human figure
questions.explanation: The early Sanchi reliefs depict the Buddha aniconically, through symbols like the wheel, footprints, the Bodhi tree, and an empty throne.

questions.q: The carved stories illustrated on the Sanchi gateways are largely drawn from which body of tales?
options: The Jataka tales of the Buddha's past lives
questions.explanation: Many of the torana reliefs depict Jataka stories and scenes from the Buddha's life.

questions.q: The sensuous female figures carved as brackets on the Sanchi gateways are known as what?
questions.explanation: The bracket figures are shalabhanjikas, tree-clasping yakshi (nature spirit) sculptures symbolizing fertility.

questions.q: Sanchi lies a short distance from which historic city, the ancient Vidisha region?
questions.explanation: Sanchi sits near the ancient city of Vidisha (Besnagar), a wealthy trading town that supported the monuments.

questions.q: Which broken Ashokan feature, with a famous polished four-lion capital, stands near the Great Stupa?
questions.explanation: An Ashokan pillar with a four-lion capital, in the famous Mauryan polished style, stands near the southern gateway.

questions.q: Which British officer is credited with first reporting the rediscovery of the Sanchi monuments to a Western audience in 1818?
questions.explanation: General Henry Taylor reported the site in 1818, after which it was studied and later conserved by the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: Relics traditionally associated with which two important disciples of the Buddha were discovered at the Sanchi complex?
questions.explanation: Relics attributed to Sariputta and Mahamoggallana, two chief disciples of the Buddha, were found in a stupa at Sanchi.

questions.q: Which national government body is responsible for the maintenance and conservation of the Sanchi monuments today?
questions.explanation: The Sanchi monuments are protected and maintained by the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: In which year were the Buddhist Monuments at Sanchi inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List?
questions.explanation: The Buddhist Monuments at Sanchi were added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1989.

questions.q: Approximately what is the diameter of the main dome of the Great Stupa at Sanchi?
questions.explanation: The enlarged Great Stupa is roughly 36 metres in diameter and about 16 metres high.

questions.q: Inscriptions at Sanchi record that much of the construction was funded by what source?
options: Numerous small donations from ordinary devotees and craftspeople
Trade taxes levied by the Mughals
questions.explanation: Hundreds of donor inscriptions show the gateways and railings were funded by collective gifts from monks, nuns, traders, and artisans.

questions.q: According to Buddhist tradition, Ashoka's connection to Sanchi is often linked to which family member said to be from nearby Vidisha?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Ashoka's wife Devi was from Vidisha, helping explain his patronage of the Sanchi area.

questions.q: On the Sanchi toranas, the early script used in the donor and label inscriptions is which writing system?
questions.explanation: The inscriptions at Sanchi are written in the Brahmi script, the script also used in Ashoka's edicts.

questions.q: Besides the Great Stupa (Stupa No. 1), the Sanchi hill also preserves remains of which other type of Buddhist structures?
options: Smaller stupas, pillars, temples, and monasteries (viharas)
questions.explanation: The hilltop complex includes several smaller stupas, an Ashokan pillar, later Gupta-era temples, and monastery (vihara) ruins.

questions.q: One of the earliest free-standing structural temples in India, built in the Gupta period, survives at Sanchi as which monument?
questions.explanation: Temple 17 at Sanchi, dated to the early Gupta period (c. 5th century CE), is among the earliest surviving free-standing stone temples in India.

questions.q: Which 20th-century restoration, led by Sir John Marshall of the ASI, helped stabilize and reconstruct the Sanchi monuments?
questions.explanation: Sir John Marshall directed major conservation of Sanchi between roughly 1912 and 1919, restoring the gateways and dome.
